title: erigeron subtrinervis subsp. conspicuus
intro: Erigeron subtrinervis subsp. conspicuus, also known as the tall fleabane, is a plant species within the sunflower family that is native to the western United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Erigeron subtrinervis subsp. conspicuus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in the treatment of various conditions. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties.
